summary: Brought `src/melee/mp/mpisland.c` from an empty stub to a partial decompilation covering `mpIsland_8005A6F8`, `mpIsland_8005A728`, `mpIsland_8005AB54`, `mpIsland_8005AC14`, `mpIsland_8005AC8C`, and `mpIsland_8005ACE8`, reported as 33.59% of the 3.52 KB file. The PR also introduced initial `mp` unknown structs, corrected or added `mpLib` prototypes needed by the new code, adjusted one `mpColl` callsite for the changed `mpLib_8004F008` signature, added a platform `ushort` typedef, and marked related scratch entries as `OK`. There were no review comments in the provided PR slice.
what_changed: Added 334 lines of implementation to `src/melee/mp/mpisland.c`, including the global `mpIsland_80458E88` and functions `mpIsland_8005A6F8`, `mpIsland_8005A728`, `mpIsland_8005AB54`, `mpIsland_8005AC14`, `mpIsland_8005AC8C`, and `mpIsland_8005ACE8`.

Replaced placeholder `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations in `mpisland.h` with typed prototypes using `mp_UnkStruct0`, `mp_UnkStruct4`, `mp_UnkStruct5`, `Vec3`, `float`, and `bool`, and typed `mpIsland_80458E88` as `mp_UnkStruct0`.
Created `src/melee/mp/types.h` with initial definitions for `mp_UnkStruct0` through `mp_UnkStruct6` plus `struct mpisland`, using offset-style fields such as `x24`, `x28`, and array placeholders.
Added forward declarations for the new `mp` unknown structs and `mpisland` in `src/melee/mp/forward.h`.

Updated `mplib.h`: changed `mpLib_8004D17C` from `void` to `int`, changed `mpLib_8004F008` to take an `int*` result parameter and an extra final argument, and added prototypes for `mpLib_8004D164`, `mpLib_8004D16C`, and `mpLib_8004D174`.
Updated `mpcoll.c` callsite of `mpLib_8004F008` to pass the newly required extra final argument as `/* TODO */ 0`.
Added `typedef unsigned short ushort;` to `src/Runtime/platform.h` to support the new struct field declarations.
Added `<placeholder.h>` include to `lb_00B0.h`, likely to satisfy declarations used by the new `mpisland.c` include path.
Changed multiple `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` entries for addresses `0x8005A6F8`, `0x8005A728`, `0x8005AB54`, `0x8005AC14`, `0x8005AC8C`, and `0x8005ACE8` from percentage entries to `OK` entries.
smart_moves: Introduced a dedicated `mp/types.h` instead of scattering unknown `mp` struct definitions through implementation files, making later struct refinement easier.
Used `mp/forward.h` for forward typedefs so headers such as `mpisland.h` can expose typed APIs without requiring full struct definitions.
Kept partially understood layouts explicit with offset-based names and comments, preserving ABI/layout evidence while allowing functions to type-check.
Refined dependent `mpLib` prototypes only as far as needed by the new callers, including return type and pointer-result information for `mpLib_8004D17C`, `mpLib_8004D174`, and `mpLib_8004F008`.
Updated an existing `mpColl` callsite immediately after changing `mpLib_8004F008`, preventing a signature drift from breaking other code.
Left an explicit `TODO` on the new final `mpLib_8004F008` argument at the existing `mpColl` callsite, documenting that the argument is still not semantically understood.
Used `IWYU pragma: keep`/`export` comments around `mpisland.h` and `mp/types.h`, signaling that include behavior was intentional despite generated or indirect-looking dependencies.
decomp_lessons: Partial decompilation can still unlock useful header and type progress: even a 33.59% file match produced concrete prototypes, global typing, and several reusable `mp` structs.
When a callee signature changes during decomp, update all visible callsites in the same PR and annotate unknown arguments rather than hiding uncertainty.
For unknown structs, offset-preserving field names like `x24` and `x28` are preferable to invented semantic names; they keep future renames searchable and safe.
Forward declarations are useful for breaking include cycles when newly typed APIs need to mention unknown structs before their layouts are fully reliable.
A scratch-to-source migration should also clean up `scratches.txt` by moving accepted candidates to `OK`, preserving prior author/id metadata.

Keeping raw casts, integer stores into float/vector fields, duplicate-looking checks, and register comments can be acceptable in early matching work when the priority is codegen fidelity over readability.
Use `TODO` comments to isolate places where a struct rewrite or prototype parameter is known to be incomplete, as seen in `mpIsland_8005A728` and the `mpLib_8004F008` callsite.
naming_conventions: `mpIsland_8005....` is used for functions in `mpisland.c`, preserving the subsystem prefix plus address suffix convention.
`mpIsland_80458E88` names the file/global object with the subsystem prefix and data address suffix.
Unknown `mp` structs are named `mp_UnkStruct0`, `mp_UnkStruct1`, etc., and are forward-declared in `mp/forward.h` before being defined in `mp/types.h`.
Unresolved fields use offset-style names such as `x4`, `x24`, `x28`, and arrays like `x0[8]` or `x20[8]` rather than speculative semantic names.
The PR introduced `struct mpisland` in lowercase, distinct from the `mpIsland_` function/global prefix.
Platform aliases follow the existing short typedef style by adding `ushort` next to `u32`, `u64`, and `uint`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved decompiler-style local names and register comments in `mpIsland_8005A728` and `mpIsland_8005ACE8`, such as `v0`, `v33`, `// r27`, and `BYREF`, indicating an early matching-oriented translation.
Used a stack byte buffer `unsigned char v33[1524]` and `lb_8000C160(v33, 0x600u)` to mirror a large stack-clear pattern.
Used raw pointer arithmetic and casts against `mpLib` data tables, for example `(v1 + 8 * v12)` and `*(short*) (*v15 + 6)`, where struct layouts were not yet fully recovered.
Preserved exact-looking control flow including duplicate condition checks in `mpIsland_8005AC8C`, likely to maintain generated code shape.
Copied vector components in `mpIsland_8005ACE8` through integer casts such as `*(int*) &arg1->x = arg0->x8`, a common matching tactic when bit-exact float moves matter.
Kept an unfinished block in `mpIsland_8005A728` commented with `@todo Rewrite as a struct ptr`, showing the current match still depended on unresolved struct layout/codegen issues.
Changed `mpLib_8004F008` to expose an `int*` output parameter, enabling `mpIsland_8005AC14` to pass `&i` and then look up the island with `mpIsland_8005AB54(i)`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested follow-up was available; lessons are inferred only from the diff and PR report.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or refine `mp_UnkStruct0` through `mp_UnkStruct6` in `src/melee/mp/types.h`.
Find later PRs that complete or rewrite `mpIsland_8005A728`, especially around the `@todo Rewrite as a struct ptr` block.
Find later changes to the final argument of `mpLib_8004F008` and the `/* TODO */ 0` callsite in `mpcoll.c`.
Find later semantic names for `mpIsland_80458E88` and `struct mpisland`.
Find other `mp` PRs that use `mpLib_8004D164`, `mpLib_8004D174`, or `mpLib_8004D17C` to recover shared map collision table layouts.
